title: PROP-021 negative-membership Tier-0 assertions (feed-restored / tag-inventory-built NOT public) are tautological
description: PROP-021 (verification-architecture.md:145) requires: 'FeedRestored and TagInventoryBuilt are NOT in the PublicDomainEvent union ... verify PublicDomainEvent union membership via TypeScript type assertion'. This was the explicit retrofit for FIND-007. The implementation in pipeline.test.ts:46-51 encodes the negative direction as `type _FeedRestoredNotPublic = Extract<PublicDomainEvent, { kind: 'feed-restored' }>; const _f: _FeedRestoredNotPublic = undefined as never;` (and analogously for tag-inventory-built). Because `undefined as never` is the bottom type and is assignable to ANY target type — including a non-`never` Extract result — the assignment compiles whether `_FeedRestoredNotPublic` resolves to `never` (current correct state) OR to `{kind:'feed-restored', ...}` (regression state). If a future edit adds `FeedRestored` to the `PublicDomainEvent` union in shared/events.ts, the test will continue to pass with no compile error. The positive-direction assertion at line 56 (`const _v: _VaultScannedIsPublic = null as unknown as VaultScanned`) IS correctly load-bearing — but the negative direction, which is the substantive content of REQ-013b and the original FIND-007, is not. PROP-021's Tier-0 claim is therefore only half-enforced.
remediation: Use a type-level equality (or extends-never) check that fails when the Extract is non-never. Example: `type _IsNever<T> = [T] extends [never] ? true : false; const _feedRestoredIsNotPublic: _IsNever<_FeedRestoredNotPublic> = true;`. If `feed-restored` is added to `PublicDomainEvent`, `_IsNever<_FeedRestoredNotPublic>` becomes `false`, and the assignment `true` to `false` will fail compile. Apply the same pattern to `_TagInventoryBuiltNotPublic`.
